title: SPX Finally Pulls Back After Weeks Of Upside. Will Bulls Buy The Dip? June 8 Plan
condicion_bias: Compra de caídas (dips) tras acumulación institucional en niveles de soporte clave
setup: Failed Breakdown en 7527 donde instituciones acumulan posiciones largas tras flush down. El precio recupera el bajo previamente establecido, atrapando shorts y disparando hacia arriba.
invalida_si: Si el precio no recupera y sostiene por encima de 7527, o si cae por debajo del low de Wednesday con cierre firme
content_preview:
As readers know, the theme for the past several months has been
buy dips
. The dips vary in size, speed, duration, structure, but they all resolve the same: They get bought.
As I frequently discuss
all major rallies
in ES start on Failed Breakdowns, because Failed Breakdowns
are how
institutions accumulate
. Institutions accumulate when ES flushes hard and goes
elevator down
- losing, and then recovering a big previously set low. In doing so institutions are able to trap shorts that are chasing, use them as liquidity, then price rips the other way when the low recovers. Usually, this process correlated with an external headline shock as institutions love to use headlines for liquidity to trap shorts (or in rare cases, they/insiders are aware of headlines in advance).
Yesterday and today, ES was tested with its largest series of dips since this leg up began.
As readers know, all week ES had been rangebound
mostly 7527 to 7620. Wednesday evening, went
elevator down
from 7600+ down to 7525.
Whenever we see a sell like this, we know to begin watch for signs of institutional accumulation so we can hop in long. I wrote Wednesday at 4pm: “7527 is below there, and this is where things get interesting. We set a nice low there at 10am last Thursday from which we rallied to the current highs. The Failed Breakdown of this low is actionable.” Thursday morning we saw exactly this. Wednesday evening we tested 7527 and built out a shelf there. Around 7:40AM, we trapped below that shelf by a
